feature.name: Customers Migration Requirements
feature.description: Document requirements for migrating Customers feature from FileMaker to Supabase

standing_constraints: Follow Backend Change Protocol - no direct database modifications
Include code references with file:line format
Document all edge cases and failure scenarios
Provide concrete examples for API contracts

tasks.title: Create requirements folder structure
tasks.description: Create requirements/customers/ directory and README.md overview file
tasks.implementation_notes: Verified the existing requirements structure by reviewing 'CLAUDE.md' and inspecting 'requirements/customers' alongside other requirement folders to confirm the standard pattern. Confirmed no implementation changes were needed because 'requirements/customers/README.md' already existed and was comprehensive (245 lines), with supporting docs including 'acceptance-criteria.md', 'api-contracts.md', 'authorization.md', 'current-implementation.md', 'data-model-mapping.md', and 'migration-plan.md'. Key decision: mark the task complete with no file edits since the directory and overview documentation were already in place and aligned with the established template.

tasks.title: Document current FileMaker implementation
tasks.description: Analyze and document src/api/customers.js, customerService.js, useCustomer.js hook and create current-implementation.md showing call flows
tasks.implementation_notes: Enhanced the existing 'requirements/customers/current-implementation.md' by incorporating newly discovered dual-environment behavior where 'src/api/fileMaker.js' detects FileMaker WebViewer vs web app mode and routes calls either through the fm-gofer bridge or a backend API proxy via 'callBackendAPI()'. Added documentation for the backend FileMaker proxy '/filemaker/*' endpoints, updated data flow diagrams to show both execution paths, and expanded code references across 'src/api/customers.js', 'src/services/customerService.js', 'src/hooks/useCustomer.js', and 'src/hooks/useSupabaseCustomer.js'. Documented key security decisions and risks around the HMAC-SHA256 auth flow, including the issue of 'VITE_SECRET_KEY' exposure in client-side code, then verified the build succeeds with only pre-existing warnings and updated task status accordingly.

tasks.title: Investigate Supabase schema
tasks.description: Query Supabase for customers and related tables (customer_contacts, customer_email, customer_phone, customer_address, customer_sales) and document structure
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive Supabase schema documentation for customers and related tables, including foreign keys, constraints, indexes, enum usage, and data quality/multi-tenancy findings, based on direct SSH queries against the 'supabase-db' Postgres container. Added a 500+ line deliverable at '.devflow/tasks/customers-migration-requirements/supabase-schema.md' and updated '.devflow/tasks/customers-migration-requirements/tasks.json' to mark TSK0003 as done with summary notes. Key decisions highlighted include calling out missing indexes and RLS needs, identifying schema inconsistencies (enum case mismatch, 'customer_contacts.is_primary' varchar), and documenting implications of global uniqueness constraints on email/phone and nullable 'customers.organization_id'. Verified the build still succeeds after documentation changes.

tasks.title: Map FileMaker fields to Supabase schema
tasks.description: Create data-model-mapping.md documenting field-by-field mapping from devCustomers layout to Supabase tables with data types and constraints
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive FileMaker to Supabase field mapping spec by creating 'requirements/data-model-mapping.md', built from schema discovery via code inspection of 'src/components/customers/CustomerForm.jsx' and 'src/services/customerService.js' (with additional references in 'src/hooks/useSupabaseCustomer.js' and 'src/api/customers.js'). The mapping documents 21 FileMaker fields from the 'devCustomers' layout to Supabase tables, including transformation rules (boolean, timestamps, UUID preservation, normalization) and highlights key migration risks like duplicate unique constraints, required address fields, unmapped fields, and the '__ID' vs 'recordId' inconsistency. Important decisions were deferred but captured as change requests, notably how to set 'organization_id' and proposed backend schema additions such as 'customers.is_active', JSONB buckets for financial data and encrypted credentials, and a few missing columns and indexes. Verified the build still compiles (only pre-existing warnings) and updated 'tasks.json' to mark the work complete.

tasks.title: Define API endpoint contracts
tasks.description: Create api-contracts.md specifying all required backend endpoints (list, get, create, update, delete, toggle status) with request/response examples
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive API contracts document at '.devflow/tasks/customers-migration-requirements/api-contracts.md' specifying 11 backend endpoints for the Customers migration with complete request/response examples, validation rules, error handling, and edge case documentation. The 1000+ line specification covers: (1) Core CRUD operations (List, Get by ID, Create, Update, Toggle Status, Delete) with full request/response examples and error scenarios; (2) Specialized endpoints (Search, Batch Create, Statistics, Validate) for migration support; (3) Authentication/authorization with HMAC-SHA256 and organization scoping via RLS; (4) Detailed edge case handling (duplicate emails/phones, concurrent updates, partial failures, address validation, organization assignment, FileMaker ID reconciliation); (5) Frontend integration examples showing refactoring from FileMaker to backend API; (6) Performance considerations, rate limiting, pagination, and idempotency patterns. Key design decisions documented include atomic transactions for related table updates, soft delete with restore capability, optimistic locking for concurrent updates, and migration-specific batch operations. Verified build succeeds (only pre-existing warnings) and marked task complete in tasks.json. Document status: Requirements Complete - Ready for Backend Team Review.

tasks.title: Document authorization requirements
tasks.description: Create authorization.md covering RLS policies, organization scoping, user roles, and permission requirements

tasks.title: Analyze dual-write patterns
tasks.description: Review dualWriteService.js and useSupabaseCustomer.js to understand current FileMaker→Supabase synchronization behavior

tasks.title: Design ID reconciliation strategy
tasks.description: Document how to map FileMaker __ID and recordId to Supabase UUIDs, including lookup tables or migration mappings

tasks.title: Create migration plan
tasks.description: Create migration-plan.md with data backfill strategy, cutover plan, rollback procedures, and validation steps

tasks.title: Document edge cases
tasks.description: Identify and document edge cases: concurrent updates, partial failures, network issues, orphaned records, duplicate detection

tasks.title: Define acceptance criteria
tasks.description: Create acceptance-criteria.md with test cases, success metrics, performance requirements, and validation procedures

tasks.title: Review and validate documentation
tasks.description: Review all documentation for completeness, consistency, and actionability for backend team
